title: Stabilize active releases
description: Current development is focused on stabilizing existing projects, improving public presentation, and tightening release consistency across platforms.
items: Improve CurseForge and GitHub presentation consistency
Refine screenshots, metadata, and release descriptions
Address high-priority bug reports before feature expansion

title: Expand core utility mods
description: The next development cycle focuses on improving utility-focused mods with practical features that maintain lightweight architecture.
items: Improve Pocket Filter workflow and usability
Expand Chunk Lock compatibility and server tooling

title: Evaluate new project candidates
description: New projects are evaluated against maintenance burden, long-term support cost, compatibility fragmentation, and practical gameplay value.
items: Evaluate Ghostlay as a focused utility release
Reject concepts that require excessive balancing

title: Selective loader and version expansion
description: Version support expands selectively based on ecosystem stability, player demand, and long-term maintainability.
items: Maintain Forge 1.20.1 as the primary stable baseline
Avoid supporting every Minecraft release immediately
Keep support targets realistic for long-term maintenance

title: Improve GOGLEOX Foundry platform
description: The Foundry website is being rebuilt around structured JSON-driven content and maintainable static architecture.
items: Build roadmap and version outlook systems
